meta.name: Udon Noodles (Kake Udon Base + Toppings Options)
meta.subtitle: Springy udon in a light dashi-soy broth with classic garnishes

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Designed as a flexible base: swap broth strength and toppings without changing core steps.

signals.notes: Best served immediately; noodles soften if held too long in broth.

tasteProfile.balanceNotes: Adjust soy and mirin to taste; use lighter broth for delicate toppings.

notes: For gluten-free: use certified GF udon or rice noodles; verify soy sauce is GF (tamari).
For vegetarian/vegan: use kombu + shiitake dashi (or vegan instant dashi).

ingredients.label: Udon noodles (fresh or dried)
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen udon preferred for springy texture; dried works too.

ingredients.label: Dashi stock (prepared)
ingredients.notes: About 3 cups. Use prepared dashi, instant dashi, or kombu+bonito dashi.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: About 2 Tbsp; adjust to taste and dashi strength.

ingredients.label: Mirin
ingredients.notes: About 1 Tbsp; adds gentle sweetness and gloss.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp; optional if mirin is sweet enough.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Only if needed; dashi + soy often provide enough salt.

ingredients.label: Nori (seaweed), shredded or cut into strips
Tempura (shrimp or vegetables)
ingredients.notes: Add on top just before serving so it stays crisp.

ingredients.label: Spinach (or baby greens)
ingredients.notes: Quick blanch in noodle water or broth.

steps.title: Make the udon broth (kake-jiru)
instructions: In a saucepan, combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and (optional) sugar.
Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low and keep warm (do not aggressively boil).
Taste and adjust: add a splash more soy for salt/umami, or a touch more mirin/sugar for sweetness.

steps.title: Boil the udon noodles
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
Cook udon according to package directions until springy and cooked through.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking, especially for fresh/frozen noodles.
checks: Noodles are tender but still springy

steps.title: Rinse, drain, and portion noodles
instructions: Rinse briefly under hot water to remove excess starch (or cold water then re-warm in broth if noodles are very starchy).
Divide noodles between two serving bowls.

steps.title: Warm toppings (optional) and assemble bowls
instructions: If using mushrooms or spinach, warm/blanch them quickly (in broth or noodle water).
Arrange toppings over noodles: sliced kamaboko, egg halves, spinach, mushrooms, or tempura.
Keep tempura last so it stays crisp.

steps.title: Ladle broth and finish
instructions: Ladle hot broth over noodles until submerged but not overflowing.
Top with sliced green onion and nori.
Finish with a pinch of shichimi togarashi if desired. Serve immediately.

serveWith.label: Miso soup (if serving smaller udon bowls)

pairings.notes: Kake udon is a light noodle bowl; pair with pickles or a small side for a complete meal.

refrigerator.notes: Store noodles and broth separately; reheat broth gently and re-warm noodles briefly in hot water.

freezer.notes: Texture suffers; better to freeze broth only (without noodles) if needed.

methods: Broth: stovetop on low until steaming
Noodles: dip in hot water 30–60 seconds, then drain
reheat.notes: Do not hold noodles in hot broth for long; they soften.

planner.weeknightNotes: Use frozen udon + instant dashi for a fast weeknight bowl.

notes: Broth strength varies by dashi brand and soy sauce. Adjust to your taste.
For tempura udon: keep tempura separate and place on top at serving so it stays crisp.
If using dried udon, cook a little longer and rinse well to remove starch.
Add tempura on top right before serving.
Keep broth slightly lighter so tempura flavor stands out.
Top with sweet-simmered aburaage (inari tofu pouch).
Increase mirin slightly for a warmer sweetness.
Use kombu + shiitake dashi or vegan instant dashi.
Use tamari (GF) if needed and omit fish-based toppings.

summary: A flexible Japanese udon noodle bowl with light dashi-soy broth and customizable toppings—perfect for fast weeknight comfort.

foodSafety: Serve noodles promptly; avoid leaving cooked noodles in hot broth for long periods.
Refrigerate leftovers within 2 hours; store noodles and broth separately.
allergenNotes: Contains wheat (udon) and soy (soy sauce); may contain fish (dashi).
